prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='FT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.69) and high seasonality variance (0.89), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA leads in trend tracking, FT_svr excels at seasonality, while ARIMA dominates overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, suggesting per-horizon selection could help. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "THETA",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "ETS", "THETA", "svr", "DWT_svr", "catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "FT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "rf", "DWT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6934,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.69) and high seasonality variance (0.89), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA leads in trend tracking, FT_svr excels at seasonality, while ARIMA dominates overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, suggesting per-horizon selection could help. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (small k for n_windows=3). Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seasonality variance (0.89) and trend/seasonality champion divergence. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 uses recommended shrinkage from knobs. RMSE spread ratio=0.6934 justifies rmse_focus preset."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No ≥4 of same type. All candidates are safe: baselines use no fitting, selection uses past-window validation data, weighted uses inverse-RMSE from past windows only. Universe leaderboards show better performers but adding more weighted candidates risks leakage; current set is sufficient for diversity and safety.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.69) and high seasonality variance (0.89), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA leads in trend tracking, FT_svr excels at seasonality, while ARIMA dominates overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, suggesting per-horizon selection could help. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (small k for n_windows=3). Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seasonality variance (0.89) and trend/seasonality champion divergence. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 uses recommended shrinkage from knobs. RMSE spread ratio=0.6934 justifies rmse_focus preset.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No ≥4 of same type. All candidates are safe: baselines use no fitting, selection uses past-window validation data, weighted uses inverse-RMSE from past windows only. Universe leaderboards show better performers but adding more weighted candidates risks leakage; current set is sufficient for diversity and safety.
